thig13.2:1.1:
Rohiṇī’s father:
You go to sleep saying,
“Contemplatives.”
thig13.2:1.4: No doubt you will be a contemplative.
thig13.2:2.4: Why do you hold contemplatives dear?
thig13.2:3.1: They don’t like to work, they’re lazy,
thig13.2:3.2: living off what’s given by others,
thig13.2:3.3: full of hankerings, wanting delicious things:
thig13.2:3.4:
Why do you hold contemplatives dear?
Rohiṇī:
thig13.2:5.1: They do like to work, they’re not lazy.
thig13.2:5.4: That’s why I hold contemplatives dear.
thig13.2:6.1: They rid themselves of the three evil roots,
thig13.2:6.4: That’s why I hold contemplatives dear.
thig13.2:7.4: That’s why I hold contemplatives dear.
thig13.2:8.4: That’s why I hold contemplatives dear.
thig13.2:9.3: they teach the goal & the Dhamma:
thig13.2:9.4: That’s why I hold contemplatives dear.
thig13.2:10.4: That’s why I hold contemplatives dear.
thig13.2:11.3: they discern the end of suffering:
thig13.2:11.4: That’s why I hold contemplatives dear.
thig13.2:12.2: they don’t turn to look back at anything.
thig13.2:12.3: How free from concern they go!
thig13.2:12.4: That’s why I hold contemplatives dear.
thig13.2:13.1: They don’t store in a granary,
thig13.2:13.3: They hunt [only] for what’s already cooked:
thig13.2:13.4: That’s why I hold contemplatives dear.
thig13.2:14.3: They live off whatever is present:
thig13.2:14.4: That’s why I hold contemplatives dear.
thig13.2:15.1: Having gone forth from different families
thig13.2:15.3: still they hold one another dear:
thig13.2:15.4:
That’s why I hold contemplatives dear.
Rohiṇī’s father:
thig13.2:16.2: were you born in our family.
thig13.2:16.3: You have conviction in the Buddha & Dhamma,
thig13.2:16.4: and strong respect for the Saṅgha.
thig13.2:19.1: go to the Buddha for refuge,
thig13.2:19.4:
That will lead to your benefit.
Rohiṇī’s father:
thig13.2:20.1: I go to the Buddha for refuge;
thig13.2:20.2: I go to the Dhamma & Saṅgha.
thig13.2:20.4: That will lead to my benefit. * * *
thig13.2:21.1: Before, I was a kinsman to Brahmā;
